Title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- System is live; PostgreSQL database is accessible.
- No duplicate email exists in the system.
Test Data:
- First Name: "John"
- Last Name: "Doe"
- Email: "john.doe@example.com"
- Password: "SecurePass123!"
- Confirm Password: "SecurePass123!"
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Register activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter First Name, Last Name, Email, Password, and Confirm Password.
3. Click the **Submit** button.
Expected Result:
- User account is created in PostgreSQL.
- System redirects to **Login activity** with a success message.
- Notification center receives a confirmation email (if applicable).

Title: Registration failure with invalid email format
Test Data:
- First Name: "Jane"
- Last Name: "Smith"
- Email: "jane.smith" (invalid format)
- Password: "Pass123"
- Confirm Password: "Pass123"
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Register activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter invalid email and other valid fields.
3. Click the **Submit** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Invalid email format."
- No new record is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the registration page.

Title: Registration failure with duplicate email
Preconditions: - A user with email "duplicate@example.com" already exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Data:
- First Name: "Alice"
- Last Name: "Johnson"
- Email: "duplicate@example.com" (duplicate)
- Password: "Pass456"
- Confirm Password: "Pass456"
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Register activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter duplicate email and other valid fields.
3. Click the **Submit** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Email already registered."
- No new record is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the registration page.

Title: Successful login with valid credentials
Preconditions: - A user with email "john.doe@example.com" and password "SecurePass123!" exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Login activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter valid email and password.
3. Click the **Login** button.
Expected Result:
- User is authenticated and redirected to the **Profile** activity.
- Notification center may display a welcome message.
- Session is created in PostgreSQL (or backend).

Title: Login failure with incorrect password
Preconditions: - A user with email "john.doe@example.com" exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Login activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter valid email and incorrect password.
3. Click the **Login** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Invalid email or password."
- No session is created.
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Login failure with non-existent email
Preconditions: - No user with email "nonexistent@example.com" exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Login activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter non-existent email and any password.
3. Click the **Login** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Invalid email or password."
- No session is created.
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Successful newsletter subscription
Preconditions:
- User is logged in (e.g., "john.doe@example.com").
- Newsletter activity is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Newsletter activity** from the Environment.
2. Select "Tech Updates" newsletter.
3. Click the **Subscribe** button.
Expected Result:
- User is subscribed to "Tech Updates" in PostgreSQL.
- Notification center sends a confirmation email.
- Flash activity displays: "Subscribed successfully!"

Title: Newsletter subscription failure with invalid email
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Newsletter activity** from the Environment.
2. Enter invalid email and select a newsletter.
3. Click the **Subscribe** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Invalid email format."
- No subscription is recorded in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the newsletter page.

Title: Successful event creation by authenticated user
Preconditions:
- User "john.doe@example.com" is logged in.
- **Create event** activity is accessible.
Test Data:
- Event Title: "Tech Conference 2023"
- Date: "2023-12-15"
- Time: "09:00 AM"
- Location: "Online"
- Description: "Annual tech conference with keynote speakers."
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Create event** activity from the Environment.
2. Fill in all event details.
3. Click the **Create** button.
Expected Result:
- Event is created in PostgreSQL with creator ID linked to "john.doe@example.com".
- Notification center sends a confirmation to the creator.
- Flash activity displays: "Event created successfully!"
- Event appears in the **Join/View event** list.

Title: Event creation failure with missing required field
Test Data:
- Event Title: "" (empty)
- Date: "2023-12-15"
- Time: "09:00 AM"
- Location: "Online"
- Description: "Annual tech conference."
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Create event** activity from the Environment.
2. Leave **Event Title** empty and fill other fields.
3. Click the **Create** button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Event title is required."
- No event is created in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the event creation page.

Title: Successful event joining by authenticated user
Preconditions:
- User "john.doe@example.com" is logged in.
- An event "Tech Conference 2023" exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Join/View event** activity from the Environment.
2. Search for "Tech Conference 2023" and select it.
3. Click the **Join** button.
Expected Result:
- User is added to the event's attendee list in PostgreSQL.
- Notification center sends a confirmation to the joiner and event creator.
- Flash activity displays: "Joined event successfully!"
- Event appears in the user's **Profile** activity under "Joined Events."

Title: Event joining failure by unauthenticated user
Preconditions:
- No user is logged in.
- An event "Tech Conference 2023" exists in PostgreSQL.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Join/View event** activity from the Environment.
2. Search for "Tech Conference 2023" and attempt to join.
Expected Result:
- System redirects to the **Login activity** with a message: "Login required to join events."
- No join record is created in PostgreSQL.

Title: View and update user profile successfully
Test Data:
- Current First Name: "John"
- Current Last Name: "Doe"
- Updated First Name: "Jonathan"
- Updated Last Name: "Doe-Smith"
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Profile** activity from the Environment.
2. Click **Edit Profile**.
3. Update First Name and Last Name.
4. Click **Save Changes**.
Expected Result:
- Profile is updated in PostgreSQL.
- Flash activity displays: "Profile updated successfully!"
- Updated details are reflected in the **Profile** activity.

Title: Profile update failure with empty fields
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Profile** activity from the Environment.
2. Click **Edit Profile**.
3. Leave all fields empty and click **Save Changes**.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "First name and last name are required."
- No changes are saved in PostgreSQL.
- User remains on the profile edit page.

Title: Successful system logout
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the **Exit** activity from the Environment (e.g., via a logout button).
2. Confirm logout if prompted.
Expected Result:
- User session is terminated in PostgreSQL/backend.
- System redirects to the **Login activity** or **Environment** home.
- Flash activity displays: "Logged out successfully!"

Title: Verify notification receipt after event joining
Preconditions:
- User "john.doe@example.com" is logged in.
- An event "Tech Conference 2023" exists with creator "alice@example.com".
Test Steps:
1. User "john.doe@example.com" joins "Tech Conference 2023" (via **Join/View event**).
2. Navigate to the **Notification center** from the Environment.
Expected Result:
- Notification center displays:
  - "You joined Tech Conference 2023" (to joiner).
  - "New attendee: john.doe@example.com" (to creator).
- Notifications are stored in PostgreSQL with read/unread status.

Title: Verify flash message display after newsletter subscription
Test Steps:
1. Subscribe to "Tech Updates" via **Newsletter activity**.
2. Check the flash activity area (e.g., top of the page).
Expected Result:
- Flash activity displays: "Subscribed to Tech Updates successfully!"
- Message disappears after 5 seconds
